title: Motorized tackle system for fishing
abstract: A powered fishing lure includes a lower body forming a fluidly sealed cavity; a battery carried within the cavity of the lower body; an upper body forming a fluidly sealed interior area; an electric motor carried within the fluidly sealed interior area and conductively coupled to the battery; a propeller positioned outside the upper body and secured to the electric motor via a drive shaft; and a hook secured to the lower body.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: A powered fishING lure, comprising: a lower body forming a fluidly sealed cavity; a battery carried within the cavity of the lower body; an upper body forming a fluidly sealed interior area; an electric motor carried within the fluidly sealed interior area and conductively coupled to the battery; a propeller positioned outside the upper body and secured to the electric motor via a drive shaft; and a hook secured to the lower body; wherein the propeller drives the fish lure through the water. The lure of claim 1, further comprising: a hinge sandwiched between the lower body and the upper body; wherein the hinge allows pivoting movement of the lower body relative to the upper body. The lure of claim 1, further comprising: a float secured to upper body via a cable; wherein the float is configured to keep the fish lure floating at a predetermined depth relative to a water surface. The lure of claim 1, further comprising: a switch secured to the lower body and configured to activate the electric motor. The lure of claim 1, further comprising: a microcontroller carried within the cavity of the lower body and conductively coupled to the propeller; wherein the microcontroller is configured to control the rotational movement of the propeller to mimic the movement of a fish swimming in the water. A method to mimic the movement of a fish in water, comprising: 

 providing the system of claim 7; programming the microcomputer with data so as to mimic the movement of the fish water; and controlling the movement of the propeller via the microcomputer. The method of claim 6, further comprising: catching a fish via the hook secured to the lower body.
